title: Tool Choice, Evidence, Actions, and Approval Boundaries · Prompt Defense
summary: Design a tool-using investigation that reads evidence before changing state. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a safe tool-use decision protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Write tool descriptions and schemas that make capability, inputs, outputs, and side effects unambiguous.
Sequence read-only discovery before mutations unless the user explicitly requests an immediate safe action.
Base the next decision on actual tool results and distinguish inference from observation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: prompt tools actions. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: prompt tools actions. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for handle timeouts, partial results, retries, and alternate evidence without looping blindly.

content.text: Handle timeouts, partial results, retries, and alternate evidence without looping blindly. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Compare plausible approaches against the decision, constraints, evidence quality, and cost of being wrong.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt tools actions. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Tool Failure Recovery, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a safe tool-use decision protocol.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Repeating the identical failing tool call is a robust recovery strategy. This fails the Tool Failure Recovery contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Anthropic Prompting Best Practices (Current Claude model guidance) — Controls clear instructions, examples, structure, reasoning, tools, and model-specific migration cautions.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle timeouts, partial results, retries, and alternate evidence without looping blindly.

hints: Name the Tool Failure Recovery precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: prompt tools actions. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Repeating the identical failing tool call is a robust recovery strategy.

stimulus.title: Tool Failure Recovery failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Repeating the identical failing tool call is a robust recovery strategy.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle timeouts, partial results, retries, and alternate evidence without looping blindly.

options.text: Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Tool Description evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Tool Description assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Tool Failure Recovery applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Handle timeouts, partial results, retries, and alternate evidence without looping blindly.

hints: Name the Tool Failure Recovery precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.
